title: Your spring reading list, mapped
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston.com offers a spring reading guide with 16 new book releases organized on a mood grid.
The reading matrix categorizes books by pace and tone for varied reading preferences.
Readers can sign up to receive the full guide and top book picks via email.

title: American Express is opening a lounge with a terrace at Logan Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: American Express will open a two-story Centurion Lounge with a terrace at Logan Airport's Terminal C in 2029.
The lounge, inspired by Boston's character, will offer amenities like premium food, WiFi, and local artwork.
Massport approved a $75 million budget for the lounge shell; American Express will spend $391 million including rent.

title: ‘Thank you for 8 great years!’: Fenway Johnnie’s has closed
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Fenway Johnnie's at 96 Brookline Ave. closed after 8 years near Fenway Park.
The closure announcement surprised many customers, with some thinking it was an April Fools' joke.
The bar and restaurant offered potato skins, burgers, and a Fenway sausage, among other items.

title: Jayson Tatum reacts to Duke’s buzzer-beater loss to UConn: ‘I was sick’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed disappointment after Duke's loss to UConn in the NCAA Elite Eight.
Tatum, now an advisor to Duke, praised the team and coach Jon Scheyer for an impressive season.
UConn's Braylon Mullins hit a game-winning shot, advancing the Huskies to the Final Four.

title: Boston man charged with sexually assaulting MBTA bus driver
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston resident Michael Vandenbroeke, 41, charged with indecent assault and battery against an MBTA bus driver.
Vandenbroeke allegedly assaulted the driver on March 26 after boarding at Massachusetts Avenue and Washington Street.
The suspect, with prior violent convictions, was arrested at Nubian Square station; bail set at $500.

title: The best movies on Netflix right now (April 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "28 Years Later: The Bone Temple" featuring Ralph Fiennes is a new addition to Netflix's movie lineup.
Critically acclaimed "Atonement" from 2007, starring James McAvoy and Keira Knightley, is now streaming on Netflix.
Classic action film "Mission: Impossible" with Tom Cruise is available on Netflix, highlighting early cerebral plots.

title: Kelsey Fitzsimmons was found not guilty. Now she’s suing.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, ex-North Andover officer, was acquitted of assault after being shot by a colleague during a restraining order service.
Fitzsimmons plans to sue the town, alleging civil rights violations and negligence, after her acquittal last week.
The shooting occurred when officers served a restraining order procured by her ex-fiancé, claiming she was a danger.

title: A church turned 6-BR home in Nahant — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A converted 6-bedroom church in Nahant was built in 1851 and offers ocean views and an in-law apartment.
The property measures 5,969 square feet and includes 3 full bathrooms, with public sewer and water services.
The listing price options for the converted church are $1.6 million, $1.8 million, and $2 million.

title: Should I DIY repair damage to a brand-new vehicle?
author: John Paul
bullets: AAA Northeast's Car Doctor advises that leaving a damaged side mirror on a leased 2026 Honda CR-V might incur lease penalties.
Weather should not affect the operation of the mirror's electronics, but repairing a damaged mirror can preserve vehicle value.
DIY repairs, using a homemade mold and factory paint, are feasible but could be challenging for non-experienced individuals.

title: Man seen walking nude around N.H. town was suffering from ‘possible medical condition,’ police say
author: John Waller
bullets: Police in Bedford, New Hampshire, say a nude man seen Thursday morning may be suffering from a medical condition.
Officers investigated reports of a nude man walking from 2 a.m. to 3 a.m. across driveways in Bedford.
Police released partial photos of the man, potentially linked to prior similar incidents, seeking public assistance.
